content: We can frame all autonomous biological and chemical agents, from enzymes to viral pathogens, as naturally evolved "Neural Turing Machines" that store interaction memory in dynamic molecular structures and execute learned algorithmic response routines to manipulate their environment based on sensory input. This perspective re-frames biological agency as a form of natural, differentiable memory-augmented computation that aligns with how engineered NTMs infer and execute generalizable algorithms from observed interaction patterns.
